definition: The current credit cycle shows Minsky speculative-to-Ponzi transition characteristics in leveraged lending and private credit. Covenant-lite issuance >90% of leveraged loans, PIK toggle features in private credit, private credit AUM doubling from $800B to $1.8T (2019-2026), Russell 2000 >40% unprofitable, private debt-to-GDP ~150%. The Kalecki fiscal floor may extend the speculative phase beyond historical analogues, but the composition shift toward riskier structures is clear.
evidence: PIK toggle features in private credit
open_questions: Does the Kalecki fiscal floor prevent or merely delay the speculative-to-Ponzi transition?
Can Minsky classification be applied to self-funded entities (hyperscalers) or only to debt-funded ones?
What is the trigger for the transition from fragility to crisis given the fiscal backstop?
